name: Perplexity
summary: Fast web-native AI research with a public-facing product direction that increasingly spills into APIs and device integrations.
latestTrackedChange: Perplexity's March 2026 blog leaned into the thesis that AI becomes the computer, following February announcements about Perplexity APIs and Perplexity Computer.
verdict: Useful when speed matters more than perfect provenance depth.

name: Hugging Face
summary: The strongest public hub for models, datasets, Spaces, open-source research, and fast-moving AI tooling infrastructure.
latestTrackedChange: The April 2026 Hub changelog added Kernels and expanded agent traces, which keeps pushing Hugging Face deeper into agent-friendly infrastructure.
verdict: Still the clearest public home for open-source AI activity.

name: n8n
summary: Workflow automation with a strong self-hosted story, useful AI integrations, and enough openness to stay flexible under real operator control.
latestTrackedChange: The March 2026 release line kept moving quickly, while docs and blog coverage emphasized external secrets support and steady 1.12x releases.
verdict: Best when you want automation depth without surrendering the whole stack to a hosted black box.

name: Make
summary: A polished hosted automation platform that is shipping quickly into agents, credential collection, and MCP-shaped workflows.
latestTrackedChange: April 2026 opened the Make CLI to all users, while Q1 shipped MCP toolboxes, credential request work, and continued AI-agent product depth.
verdict: The smoother hosted choice if you want automation velocity without running your own infrastructure first.

name: OpenAI API
summary: A frontier model API stack that increasingly optimizes for real agent workflows, tool use, and professional work products.
latestTrackedChange: GPT-5.4 launched on March 5, 2026 with native computer use, tool search, and 1M-token context for longer-horizon workflows.
verdict: One of the clearest API surfaces if your automation stack needs serious tool-using model behavior.

name: Anthropic API
summary: A strong API lane for Claude-based reasoning, long context, and enterprise-facing agent workflows with a rapidly expanding model lineup.
latestTrackedChange: Anthropic's February 2026 model materials moved Claude Sonnet 4.6 into the current API story, while the API notes continue to emphasize admin and cost controls.
verdict: Strong enough to stay in every serious model comparison set, especially for long-context and structured work.

name: GitHub MCP Server
summary: GitHub's official MCP server brings repositories, issues, pull requests, actions, and related workflows directly into agent loops.
latestTrackedChange: The official server hit v1.0.0 on April 16, 2026, adding granular issue field tooling and formalizing MCP Apps feature-flag support.
verdict: A foundational MCP if your workflows already live inside GitHub.

name: Playwright MCP
summary: Structured browser automation for agents, backed by Playwright's accessibility-first interaction model and official docs.
latestTrackedChange: The latest 0.0.70 release landed on April 1, 2026 as a maintenance cut, while the official docs now frame Playwright MCP as the structured browser layer for coding agents.
verdict: A high-value specialist tool whenever real browser truth matters more than abstract reasoning.

name: GitHub CLI
summary: Still one of the best developer CLIs going, and now clearly leaning into agent skills and official extension ergonomics.
latestTrackedChange: GitHub CLI 2.90.0 shipped `gh skill` in public preview on April 16, 2026, alongside better official extension suggestions.
verdict: Still an easy default CLI recommendation for serious GitHub users.

name: Wrangler
summary: Cloudflare's command-line surface for Workers keeps absorbing more platform breadth without losing its day-to-day usefulness.
latestTrackedChange: Wrangler 4.58.0 added grouped command categories, better auth token handling, and auto type generation on dev startup in January 2026.
verdict: Best when you are already serious about the Cloudflare runtime and want the platform depth in one CLI.

name: Canvas Planner
summary: A visual planning surface for boards, campaign flow, and creative work without the drag of a generic project manager.
latestTrackedChange: The latest public-ready pass cleaned the browser surface and kept the board flow sharp without adding clutter.
verdict: A focused web app with clear utility if you want visual planning without a whole PM suite.

name: Signal Stack
summary: A publishing planner built around image, video, and queue workflows rather than generic social-media posting dashboards.
latestTrackedChange: The recent execution pass made delivery truth more explicit, but live platform integrations still depend on real credentials and approvals.
verdict: Interesting if you want an image-and-video-first planner, but still earlier than the strongest ecosystem picks.

name: Strudel Studio
summary: A browser-based music studio path that brings programmable patterns, scenes, and samples into a clearer public-facing workflow.
latestTrackedChange: The takeover pass tightened the smoke runner and kept the product moving toward a more serious music workspace.
verdict: Promising and distinctive, with more identity than polish at this stage.

name: SyncPad
summary: A private, desktop-first notes tool with live sync ambitions and a workflow that stays close to the operator.
latestTrackedChange: The browser and client surfaces are healthier, but the Electron desktop verification path is still blocked by a GPU startup issue on the manager machine.
verdict: Clearly interesting, but not yet ready to be framed like a finished desktop note system.
